# UC-22.59.8 · Propulsion / Power Management System (PMS) / Dynamic Positioning System (DPS) cyber-anomaly detection — engine-control / PMS-load / DPS-station-keeping integrity

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Anomaly, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Ships' engines, electrics, and station-keeping computers must run within safe limits. We watch every key reading — engine speed, power, dynamic positioning drift — and raise the alarm if any reading wanders outside the normal range for too long.*

---

## Description

Continuously inspects propulsion, PMS, and DPS telemetry tags from PI / OEM historians; computes deviation from per-vessel operating envelope; raises a notable on sustained out-of-envelope deviation.

## Value

A 2023 case study at IACS established that 60% of newbuild DP-2 vessels had at least one DPS network-discoverable attack surface. A successful attack on DPS station-keeping during offshore loading could cause a major-environmental-incident (oil spill) or loss of life. This UC catches sustained deviations early — both for safety and cyber.

## Implementation

(1) Wire PI / OEM historian feeds to Splunk with metric-time-series mode for high-cardinality tags. (2) Define `imo_ot_operating_envelope_lookup` per vessel per system_class per tag (e.g. main-engine-rpm envelope, DG-fuel-rack envelope, DPS-position-drift envelope). (3) Schedule UC-22.59.8 every 15 minutes; route at severity P1 for dps-drift / below-envelope on propulsion, P2 otherwise.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.59.8` archived every 15 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `imo_clause="IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s3-1"`. Every row pins (imo_number, system_class, observed_value, expected_min, expected_max, anomaly_class, anomaly_indicator).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Introduce a sustained DPS station-keeping anomaly (drift > 3 metres from set-point for > 5 minutes on a DP-2 vessel). UC-22.59.8 MUST flag anomaly_indicator=true with anomaly_class=dps-drift within 15 minutes.

### Negative scenario

Normal voyage operating envelope. UC-22.59.8 MUST record anomaly_indicator=false.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.59.3 and UC-22.59.4 in production.
- PI / AVEVA historian on the engine-room subnet with read-only data export to Splunk.
- OT operating envelope per vessel — `imo_ot_operating_envelope_lookup` — owned by the Chief Engineer and updated quarterly.

## Step 1 — Operating-envelope KV Store

```ini
[imo_ot_operating_envelope_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.imo_number = string
field.system_class = string
field.tag_name = string
field.envelope_min = number
field.envelope_max = number
field.envelope_stdev_threshold = number
field.last_reviewed = time
field.reviewer = string
```

## Step 2 — Metric ingestion

Use Splunk's metrics store on a dedicated index `metrics_maritime_ot` for high-cardinality tag streams. Configure the Splunk Add-on for OSIsoft PI System with reasonable batch sizes (vessel-fleet scale 100+ vessels x 5000+ tags/vessel = significant volume).

## Step 3 — Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.59.8: IMO OT Anomaly]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.59.8,reg=IMO-MSC-428-98,clause=IMO-MSC-FAL-Circ-3-s3-1
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 — Runbooks

**`anomaly_class=dps-drift`** on DP-2 / DP-3 (owner: Chief Engineer + Master, severity P1): Within 15 minutes, the Master assesses safety implications (proximity to platform, vessels, shore); engage Engine OEM technical support if DPS is loss-of-control suspected.

**`anomaly_class=below-envelope`** on propulsion main engine (severity P1): Could indicate fuel-rack tampering or main-engine governor compromise; the Chief Engineer engages MAN / Wartsila / Caterpillar PrimeServ within 30 minutes.

## Step 5 — Maritime-specific notes

- **DP class hierarchy**: DP-1 (single fault tolerance), DP-2 (redundant fault tolerance — typical offshore supply), DP-3 (full redundancy — drillships). Tighten thresholds per class.
- **Engine OEM cross-reference**: a cyber anomaly may have an OEM PrimeServ correlate; bake the OEM ticket-reference into the alert payload.

## SPL

```spl
(sourcetype="osisoft:pi:archive" OR sourcetype="aveva:pi:audit" OR sourcetype="wartsila:olm:telemetry" OR sourcetype="kongsberg:kpos:audit" OR sourcetype="abb:pems:audit")
    system_class IN ("propulsion","pms","dps")
| bucket _time span=15m
| stats avg(observed_value) AS observed_avg, stdev(observed_value) AS observed_stdev by imo_number, system_class, tag_name, _time
| lookup imo_ot_operating_envelope_lookup imo_number system_class tag_name OUTPUT envelope_min envelope_max envelope_stdev_threshold
| eval anomaly_class = case(
    observed_avg < envelope_min, "below-envelope",
    observed_avg > envelope_max, "above-envelope",
    observed_stdev > envelope_stdev_threshold, "high-variance",
    system_class = "dps" AND tag_name = "position-drift" AND observed_avg > 3, "dps-drift",
    1=1, null)
| eval anomaly_indicator = if(isnotnull(anomaly_class), "true", "false")
| where anomaly_indicator = "true"
| table _time imo_number system_class tag_name observed_avg envelope_min envelope_max anomaly_class anomaly_indicator
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Per-vessel anomaly count (24h). (Panel 2) Tag-level heatmap (vessel x tag x anomaly intensity). (Panel 3) DPS station-keeping drift on DP-2 / DP-3 vessels (only). (Panel 4) Top OEM platforms by anomaly volume.

## Known False Positives

1. **Heavy-weather operation**: a vessel in Beaufort 9+ may legitimately show high DPS variance; tighten threshold conditioned on a `weather_class` lookup. 2. **Cargo-operations transient**: tanker loading / discharge introduces ballast-shift transients on propulsion fuel-rack signals; allow during loading-window. 3. **Newbuild-commissioning trial**: factory-trial mode is bracketed by the Chief Engineer.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `imo_22_59_8_exceptions` with the Chief Engineer's documented approval.

## References

- [IMO MSC-FAL.1/Circ.3 Rev.2 §3.1 — Propulsion, machinery, power-control](https://wwwcdn.imo.org/localresources/en/OurWork/Security/Documents/MSC-FAL.1-Circ.3-Rev.2.pdf)
- [IACS UR E26 — Cyber resilience of ships, monitoring](https://iacs.org.uk/publications/unified-requirements/ur-e/)
- [DNV-RP-G108 — Cyber security in the oil and gas industry (DPS relevance)](https://www.dnv.com/)
